estimatePolicy: Seed ranges are broad planning estimates, not official quotes. Replace or verify them through the admin price-pack upload flow before treating them as live market prices.

japan.label: Japan
japan.notes: Default RHD source-market path for Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania.

uae.label: UAE
uae.notes: Often useful for SUVs and premium stock; steering and spec must be checked.

uk.label: UK
uk.notes: Useful RHD source for several African markets.

south-africa.label: South Africa
south-africa.notes: Useful for Zambia and southern corridors; export paperwork must be checked.

local-dealer.label: Local dealer
local-dealer.notes: Represents the local asking-price layer, not an import source.

NG.label: Imported used-car finance
NG.notes: Usually requires salary inflow proof, BVN, and a stronger down payment for older imports.

KE.label: RHD import car asset finance
KE.notes: Best fit for compliant RHD imports with KEBS and KRA paperwork ready.

GH.label: Cleared imported vehicle finance
GH.notes: Usually easier after the vehicle is fully landed and cleared.

UG.label: Used import vehicle loan
UG.notes: URa valuation certainty and cleared-logbook status usually affect approval appetite.

ZM.label: Used import and workhorse finance
ZM.notes: Pickups and work vehicles usually price better than premium imports.

TZ.label: Imported vehicle on-road finance
TZ.notes: First-registration charges and licence fees should be budgeted before approval sizing.

sourceMetadata.name: URA motor vehicle import tax calculator

NG.intro: Nigeria buyers need a practical landed-cost view because Lagos port costs, clearing delays, FX, and age eligibility can change the answer quickly.
NG.hiddenCosts: Storage, terminal handling, broker fees, valuation differences, and registration buffers often matter as much as headline duty.
NG.riskCopy: Vehicles older than the active passenger-vehicle age rule should be treated as ineligible unless a verified exception applies.

KE.intro: Kenya pages emphasize Japan-to-Mombasa workflows, RHD eligibility, KEBS inspection, and KRA valuation-pack assumptions.
KE.hiddenCosts: CRSP valuation differences, IDF/RDL/VAT stacking, inspection documents, and Mombasa-to-city transport are the big watch-outs.
KE.riskCopy: A vehicle outside the eight-year or right-hand-drive rules should not be recommended without official clearance.

GH.intro: Ghana buyers need to see official taxes separately from third-party port and clearing costs because the market often blends them together.
GH.hiddenCosts: ICUMS valuation, age penalties, local shipping-line fees, safe bond/rent, trade plates, and agent charges can move the final number.
GH.riskCopy: Right-hand-steering restrictions and age penalties should be checked before paying for a car abroad.

UG.intro: Uganda estimates lean on valuation-pack logic and corridor costs from the coast into Kampala.
UG.hiddenCosts: The gap between a quick marketplace price and URA valuation can be material, especially when the official valuation pack is incomplete.
UG.riskCopy: When the valuation pack is thin, treat results as planning estimates and keep a larger buffer.

ZM.intro: Zambia needs specific-duty and valuation-schedule friendly logic, not only percentage-of-CIF math.
ZM.hiddenCosts: ASYCUDA processing, motor vehicle fees, surtax, additional agency fees, and corridor delivery costs can all affect the on-road answer.
ZM.riskCopy: Schedule freshness matters. If the rule pack is stale, preview against the latest ZRA schedule before publishing a quote.

TZ.intro: Tanzania pages split customs costs from first-registration and licensing costs so buyers can see where the final on-road total comes from.
TZ.hiddenCosts: Age-based excise, registration tax, licence fees, Dar port costs, and inland transport are the common surprises.
TZ.riskCopy: Used passenger vehicles around the 8-10 and over-10-year age bands should be inspected carefully for excise sensitivity.

ZA.intro: South Africa buyers can now review the same AfroTools vehicle price brackets in ZAR, using live USD exchange rates and a local-market planning band.
ZA.hiddenCosts: FX movement, inspection, shipping documents, inland delivery, storage, registration, and dealer margins can move the final on-road number.
ZA.riskCopy: This market is in directory-estimate mode until a full customs rule pack is added, so use the local currency range for planning and verify duties with a licensed clearing agent before paying.

faqs.question: Why do these pages show ranges instead of one price?
faqs.answer: Car import outcomes depend on source price, exchange rate, customs value, port timing, agent cost, registration, and local-market spread. Ranges are more honest than a single blunt number.

faqs.question: Is the local asking price an official valuation?
faqs.answer: No. Local asking ranges come from seed price packs and marketplace or dealer samples. Official valuation for customs comes from the country rule and valuation pack.

faqs.question: Can I save a watchlist?
faqs.answer: Yes. Watchlists save locally in the browser first and can be wired to account storage when the authenticated saved-quote backend is available.

marketCoverage.note: Cars display the existing USD vehicle brackets in local currency through the shared live forex container. Countries without full rule packs are labelled as directory estimates.
